location.office_time_required: 3 days in the office per week

level_signals: 4+ years of experience in business operations, workplace operations, program coordination, or similar roles

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 4+ years of experience in business operations, workplace operations, program coordination, employee experience, or similar roles.
Bring strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to summarize complex or nuanced topics clearly.
Are highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities while following through reliably.
Build trust across teams and are comfortable partnering with internal stakeholders in sensitive or nuanced situations.
Navigate ambiguity with sound judgment, curiosity, and a solutions-oriented mindset.
Have experience working in workplace, hospitality, real estate, operations, internal service environments, or cross-functional programs.
Are comfortable using project management tools, spreadsheets, and workflow systems to create clarity and momentum.
Are able to travel at minimum 1-2 times per quarter and support transportation needs across APAC, LATAM, and EMEA.

scope.surface: Transportation Program within Real Estate & Workplace (REW) organization — employee transportation across offices and regions
primary_metrics_inferred: Operational readiness for office openings and moves
core_responsibilities: Build strong working relationships across REW, including Events, Design & Construction, Food, Security, Facilities, and Workplace Operations.
Partner with teams across the company to understand transportation-related needs, coordinate next steps, and ensure transportation considerations are reflected in broader workplace planning.
Represent the Transportation team in meetings, planning discussions, and cross-functional initiatives as needed.
Gather input, questions, and feedback from employees and internal partners, summarize nuanced issues clearly, and route requests to the appropriate owner, resource, or process.
Track open items and help ensure timely follow-up across stakeholders and team priorities.
Support internal team planning through trackers, documentation, notes, timelines, and action items.
Help organize recurring business rhythms, project milestones, operational priorities, and readiness activities tied to office openings, moves, or workplace changes.
Draft clear internal communications, updates, FAQs, and guidance materials that help partners understand available transportation programs, processes, and tools.
Improve internal processes that help the team stay organized, responsive, and able to support a smooth employee transportation experience as the company grows.

green_flags: Clear scope and ownership area within a named team (Transportation, part of REW)
Relocation assistance offered to new employees
Specific cross-functional partners named, signaling an organized team structure
Role responsibilities are detailed and concrete rather than vague
red_flags: Role is an internal ops support function with limited strategic or career-growth signals in the JD
Travel requirement (1-2x per quarter to APAC, LATAM, EMEA) may be a constraint not prominently flagged in the headline

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred as Mid-Senior IC from 4+ years requirement; no explicit level stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred as internal ops/employee services; not stated)
ambiguities: The 'You might thrive in this role if you:' framing is softer than 'Required', but all bullet points read as substantive qualifications expected of candidates. Classified as must_haves (hard) because they are unconditional bullets describing the expected candidate profile without 'preferred' or 'bonus' language.
Visa sponsorship is neither confirmed nor denied in the JD; set to 'unclear'.
